name: Design
description: Iconic inventions, classic furniture, and the people who designed everyday things.

questions.q: Which French designer created the iconic Juicy Salif lemon squeezer for Alessi in 1990?
Who invented the Post-it Note for 3M in the 1970s, building on Spencer Silver's adhesive?

source.notes: Spencer Silver discovered the low-tack adhesive in 1968; Fry applied it to a bookmark in 1974.

questions.q: Who invented the Band-Aid in 1920 for his accident-prone wife?

source.notes: Dickson worked for Johnson & Johnson, who marketed the product.

questions.q: Who invented Liquid Paper (correction fluid) in 1956?

source.notes: Mother of Monkees member Michael Nesmith.

questions.q: Who created Scotch tape in 1930 for the 3M Company?
Who invented the Bunsen burner — a staple of chemistry labs?
Which French educator devised the raised-dot writing system used by the blind?

source.notes: Devised the system in 1824 at age 15.

questions.q: Who designed the original game of Scrabble in the 1930s?
Which architect-engineer designed the Statue of Liberty's exterior?

source.notes: Eiffel designed the internal iron framework.

questions.q: Who is credited with inventing the modern hand-held cell phone in 1973?

source.notes: Motorola engineer; placed the first cellular call to a Bell Labs rival.

questions.q: Who invented the first practical Ferris wheel for the 1893 Chicago World's Fair?

source.notes: Built to rival the Eiffel Tower of the 1889 Paris Exposition.

questions.q: Who invented the computer mouse, demonstrated in the 1968 'Mother of All Demos'?
Which Finnish architect designed the iconic 1957 'Tulip' chair for Knoll?

source.notes: Saarinen also designed the St. Louis Gateway Arch and the TWA Flight Center.

questions.q: Which Danish designer created the wishbone-shaped 'Y Chair' (CH24)?

source.notes: Designed in 1949 for Carl Hansen & Søn.

questions.q: Who designed the original 1949 Monopoly game's predecessor — 'The Landlord's Game'?

source.notes: Patented 1904. Charles Darrow took credit decades later.

questions.q: Who invented the kid's drawing toy Etch A Sketch in the late 1950s?

source.notes: Invented by French engineer André Cassagnes; original known as 'L'Écran Magique.'

questions.q: Who created the Magic 8 Ball — the fortune-telling toy?

source.notes: Albert C. Carter and Abe Bookman in 1950 — based on his mother's clairvoyance device.

questions.q: Who designed the original Apple Macintosh in 1984 and is widely credited as a typography pioneer?

source.notes: Kare designed Mac's original icons and fonts (Chicago, Geneva).

questions.q: Which iconic pen, introduced 1950, has sold over 100 billion units worldwide?

source.notes: Designed by the Société Bic team in 1950.

questions.q: Who founded the Bauhaus, the influential 20th-century design school in Germany?

source.notes: Bauhaus founded in 1919 in Weimar; closed by Nazis in 1933.
